name: Niger Military Coup
description: Presidential guard led by Gen. Tchiani detained President Bazoum and seized power, forming the CNSP. Bazoum remains detained at the presidential palace.

name: ECOWAS Standby Force Activation Against Niger
description: ECOWAS ordered activation of standby force after Niger junta ignored ultimatum. Mali and Burkina Faso warned intervention would be a 'declaration of war.'

name: JNIM Blockade of Timbuktu
description: JNIM imposed a complete blockade on Timbuktu, cutting off food supplies, humanitarian aid, and commercial flights. Food prices doubled.

name: Gabon Military Coup
description: Military officers seized power minutes after Ali Bongo was declared election winner, ending the Bongo family's 56-year rule.

name: Alliance of Sahel States (AES) Formation
description: Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger signed the Charter of Liptako-Gourma establishing a mutual defense alliance. Article VI: attack on one is attack on all.

name: Malian Army / Wagner Capture of Kidal
target: City of Kidal, Tuareg rebel stronghold
description: Malian army backed by Wagner mercenaries captured Kidal, ending over a decade of Tuareg rebel control. Wagner flew their flag over the fort on November 22.

name: Zaongo Village Massacre by Burkinabe Army
description: The Burkinabe army massacred at least 70 people in Zaongo village in Yatenga Province. Reports range from 70 to over 200 killed.

name: Plateau State Christmas Eve Massacres
target: 17+ rural communities in Bokkos and Barkin Ladi
description: Armed attackers systematically attacked villages across Plateau State on Christmas Eve in one of Nigeria's worst sectarian attacks.

name: Last French Troops Leave Niger
description: The last 1,500 French soldiers departed Niger, ending a decade of French counterterrorism operations in the Sahel. France also closed its embassy.

name: MINUSMA Completes Withdrawal from Mali
description: The UN completed its full withdrawal from Mali per Security Council Resolution 2690, ending 10 years of peacekeeping operations.

name: Russia's Africa Corps Deploys to Burkina Faso
description: Russia's Africa Corps deployed 100 military personnel with plans to expand to 300. Base established at Loumbila, 20 km northeast of Ouagadougou.

name: AES States Announce ECOWAS Withdrawal
description: The three AES juntas jointly announced ECOWAS withdrawal, citing sanctions and intervention threats. Withdrawal became effective January 29, 2025.

name: Nondin and Soro Village Massacres
target: Nondin and Soro villages, Yatenga Province
description: Burkinabe soldiers summarily executed at least 223 civilians, including 56 children. Human Rights Watch described it as the worst army abuse since 2015, possibly crimes against humanity.

name: Kuriga School Mass Kidnapping
target: Students at Kuriga schools, Kaduna State
description: Armed men abducted 287 students aged 8-15 from schools in Kuriga, demanding 1 billion naira ransom. All freed after 17 days.

name: Russia's Africa Corps Arrives in Niger
description: One hundred Africa Corps troops arrived in Niamey with a 'latest generation' air defense system to train Nigerien forces.

name: Niger Revokes Orano Uranium Mining License
description: Niger revoked Orano's license at the Imouraren mine (200,000 tonnes uranium). Orano halted all production at Arlit by October 31.

name: AES Confederation Treaty Signed
description: AES leaders signed a confederation treaty transforming the mutual defense pact into a full confederation with economic integration goals, free from Western influence.

name: US Forces Withdraw from Niamey Air Base 101
description: US forces completed withdrawal from Air Base 101 in Niamey following Niger's termination of the bilateral defense agreement.

name: Battle of Tinzaouaten
description: Tuareg rebels ambushed a Malian-Wagner convoy in a three-day battle. Wagner's largest known loss of life in Africa, with possibly only three Russian fighters surviving.

name: US Completes Withdrawal from Agadez Air Base 201
description: US completed withdrawal from Air Base 201 near Agadez, a $110 million drone base and the largest USAF-led construction project in history.

name: Barsalogho Massacre
description: JNIM attacked hundreds of civilians forced by the military to dig defensive trenches. The deadliest massacre in Burkinabe history and deadliest single attack of the Sahel insurgency.

name: Djibo Siege Humanitarian Emergency
description: MSF suspended operations in Djibo after recurrent attacks. Residents had less than 3L of water per day, resorted to eating leaves.

name: Attack on Chad Presidential Palace
description: 24 attackers armed with knives and machetes attempted to storm the presidential complex. President Deby was inside but unharmed.

name: AES States Formally Leave ECOWAS
description: Niger, Mali, and Burkina Faso officially left ECOWAS after a one-year notice period.

name: AES Joint Operation Yereko 2
target: Jihadist groups in Liptako-Gourma tri-border area
description: The three AES armies conducted a tripartite joint operation targeting jihadist sanctuaries in the tri-border area.

name: JNIM Fuel Blockade of Southern Mali
target: Fuel supply routes from Senegal, Guinea, Ivory Coast
description: JNIM announced a fuel blockade destroying over 300 tankers on national roads, crippling 95% of Mali's fuel imports.

name: AES Launches Unified Military Force (FU-AES)
description: The AES inaugurated a 5,000-strong unified military force with headquarters in Niamey, integrating air power, intelligence sharing, and coordinated ground operations.

name: JNIM/FLA Coordinated Attack on Kati Military Base
target: Kati military base, Defense Minister Sadio Camara
casualties: Defense Minister Camara killed; Intelligence Chief Kone killed April 26; Army Chief Diarra wounded
description: JNIM drove a car bomb into the Defense Minister's house. President Goita evacuated. Part of the largest coordinated offensive since the 2012 northern Mali capture.

name: JNIM Attack on Sevare Airfield
target: Sevare military airfield and Mopti area
casualties: Unknown; city split between government and rebel control
description: JNIM attacked the strategic Sevare airfield hosting Mali Air Force and Russian forces. The city was split under dual control.

name: Tuareg/JNIM Alliance Recaptures Kidal
description: In unprecedented alliance, Tuareg separatists and JNIM jointly recaptured Kidal, reversing the November 2023 victory. Africa Corps confirmed withdrawal on April 26.
